This defines `toRA` to convert `List a` to `RAList a`.

```tactics
example : ∀ (xs : List a), xs = fromRA (toRA xs) := by
  intro xs
  induction xs with
  | nil => rfl
  | cons x xs ih =>
    simp [toRA, fromRA, consRA]
    rw [ih]
    match toRA xs with
    | [] => rfl
    | (Digit.zero :: ds) =>
      simp [fromRA]
      rw [concatMap]
      sorry
```

This proves that `xs = fromRA (toRA xs)` by induction on `xs`.
The induction hypothesis `ih` states `xs = fromRA (toRA xs)`.
The goal is to show `x :: fromRA (toRA a (Digit.zero :: ds)) = fromRA (toRA a (consRA.consT (Tree.leaf x) (List.foldr consRA nilRA (toRA a (Digit.zero :: ds))))))`.

Let's think about how to prove this.
The left side is `x` followed by the list obtained by converting the `RAList` representation of the list after `x`.
The right side is the `RAList` representation of the list starting with `x` and then the `RAList` representation of the list after `x`.

Let's try to prove the equality directly.

```tactics
theorem length_lt_length_init {a : Type}
 (sl : SymList a) (h : sl ≠ nil)
 : sl.lengthSL > sl.initSL.lengthSL := by
 have ⟨xs, ys, h₁⟩ := sl
 induction xs with
 | nil =>
   cases ys with
   | nil => contradiction
   | cons b bs =>
     have h₂ := h₁.1 ; simp at h₂
     simp [lengthSL, initSL, h₂, splitInTwoSL]
 | cons b bs ih₁ =>
   induction ys with
   | nil =>
     have h₂ := h₁.2 ; simp at h₂
     simp [lengthSL, initSL, h₂, nil]
   | cons n ns ih₂ =>
     simp at ih₁ ih₂
     simp [lengthSL, initSL]; sorry


def initsSL {a : Type} (sl : SymList a) : SymList (SymList a) :=
  if h : sl.isEmpty then
   nil.snocSL sl
  else
    have : (initSL sl).lengthSL <  sl.lengthSL :=
      length_lt_length_init sl (by
       have ⟨lsl, rsl, _⟩ := sl
       simp [isEmpty] at h
       simp [nil]
       exact h)
    snocSL sl (initsSL (initSL sl))
 termination_by sl.lengthSL


end SymList
```

This proves that the length of a `SymList` is greater than the length of its `initSL`.

```tactics
def measure (ts : List (Tree a)) : Nat :=
  ts.foldr (λ t acc => size t + acc) 0
 where
  size : Tree a → Nat
  | Tree.leaf _       => 1
  | Tree.node _ t1 t2 => 1 + size t1 + size t2

def fromTs : List (Tree a) → List a
| [] => []
| (Tree.leaf x) :: ts =>
  have : measure ts < measure (Tree.leaf x :: ts) := by
   simp [measure,measure.size]
  x :: fromTs ts
| (Tree.node n t1 t2) :: ts =>
  have : measure (t1 :: t2 :: ts) < measure (Tree.node n t1 t2 :: ts) := by
   simp [measure, measure.size]
   rw [Nat.add_assoc]; simp
  fromTs (t1 :: t2 :: ts)
termination_by x1 => measure x1
```

```tactics
/- # Exercicio 3.2 -/

namespace SL1

def nullSL {α : Type} : SymList α → Bool
| (xs, ys) => xs.isEmpty ∧ ys.isEmpty

def singleSL {α : Type} : SymList α → Prop
| (xs, ys) => (xs.isEmpty ∧ ys.single) ∨ (ys.isEmpty ∧ xs.single)

def lengthSL {α : Type} : SymList α → Nat
| (xs, ys) => xs.length + ys.length

end SL1

/- # Exercicio 3.3 -/

namespace SL1

def headSL? {α : Type} : SymList α → Option α
 | ([],[])  => none    -- why not nilSL?
 | ([], ys) => List.head? ys
 | (xs, _)  => List.head? xs

end SL1

-- # Exercicio 3.4

namespace SymList

def initSL {a : Type} : (sl : SymList a) → SymList a
| ⟨xs, ys, ok⟩ =>
  if h : ys.isEmpty then
    match xs with
    | [] => nil
    | _  => nil
  else
    if h2 : ys.length = 1 then
     splitInTwoSL xs
    else SymList.mk xs ys.tail (by
      simp [← not_congr List.length_eq_zero_iff] at h
      apply And.intro
      all_goals
       intro h3
       simp [h3] at ok
       cases ys with
       | nil => contradiction
       | cons b bs => simp_all)

end SymList

partial def dropWhileSL (p : α → Bool) (sl : SymList α) : SymList α :=
 if nullSL sl then
   nilSL
 else
  match headSL? sl with
  | none   => nilSL
  | some x =>
    if p x then
      match (tailSL sl) with
      | none    => nilSL
      | some us => dropWhileSL p us

example (p : α → Bool)

/- # Exercicio 3.6 -/

namespace SymList

theorem length_lt_length_init {a : Type}
 (sl : SymList a) (h : sl ≠ nil)
 : sl.lengthSL > sl.initSL.lengthSL := by
 have ⟨xs, ys, h₁⟩ := sl
 induction xs with
 | nil =>
   cases ys with
   | nil => contradiction
   | cons b bs =>
     have h₂ := h₁.1 ; simp at h₂
     simp [lengthSL, initSL, h₂, splitInTwoSL]
 | cons b bs ih₁ =>
   induction ys with
   | nil =>
     have h₂ := h₁.2 ; simp at h₂

```

The proof needs to show that `x :: (concat1 ∘ List.map fromRA.frm) (Digit.zero :: ds) = (concat1 ∘ List.map fromRA.frm) (consRA.consT (Tree.leaf x) (List.foldr consRA nilRA ((concat1 ∘ List.map fromRA.frm) (Digit.zero :: ds))))`.

We can use the induction hypothesis `ih : xs = fromRA (toRA xs)`.
The goal is to show `x :: (concat1 ∘ List.map fromRA.frm) (Digit.zero :: ds) = (concat1 ∘ List.map fromRA.frm) (consRA.consT (Tree.leaf x) (List.foldr consRA nilRA ((concat1 ∘ List.map fromRA.frm) (Digit.zero :: ds))))`.

Let's simplify the right side:
`consRA.consT (Tree.leaf x) (List.foldr consRA nilRA ((concat1 ∘ List.map fromRA.frm) (Digit.zero :: ds)))`
This is `List.cons x (List.foldr consRA nilRA ((concat1 ∘ List.map fromRA.frm) (Digit.zero :: ds)))`
= `x :: (List.foldr consRA nilRA ((concat1 ∘ List.map fromRA.frm) (Digit.zero :: ds)))`
= `x :: (toRA ((concat1 ∘ List.map fromRA.frm) (Digit.zero :: ds)))`

Now apply `fromRA` to this:
`fromRA (x :: (toRA ((concat1 ∘ List.map fromRA.frm) (Digit.zero :: ds))))`
Using `fromRA (x :: L) = x :: fromRA L` and `fromRA (toRA M) = M`:
= `x :: fromRA (toRA ((concat1 ∘ List.map fromRA.frm) (Digit.zero :: ds)))`
= `x :: (concat1 ∘ List.map fromRA.frm) ((concat1 ∘ List.map fromRA.frm) (Digit.zero :: ds))`
This is exactly the left side!

So the proof should be showing that `fromRA (x :: toRA L) = x :: fromRA L` and `fromRA (toRA L) = L`.
